title: Earthy Flaky Japanese Eggs Bao
description: A savory, flaky Japanese-inspired bao dish packed with the earthy flavors of mushrooms, the richness of free-range eggs, and the crunch of sesame seeds.
instructions: In a pan over medium heat, sauté the sliced mushrooms until they release their moisture and start browning. Season with 1/2 tsp salt and 1 tsp sugar. Set aside.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs, rice vinegar, and molasses. Pour into the pan with the mushrooms and cook until the eggs are just set.
Meanwhile, prepare the bao dough according to package instructions or roll out homemade dough to 1/4 inch thickness.
Using a round cookie cutter or a glass, cut out circles of dough for the bao buns.
Brush the tops of the dough circles with vegetable oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Place a tablespoon of the egg and mushroom mixture onto the center of each dough circle. Fold the dough over to form a bun and press the edges together to seal.
Using a pastry brush, brush the tops of the buns with egg wash (beaten egg mixed with a splash of water).
Place the buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving 1 inch of space between each bun.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let the buns cool for 5 minutes before serving.
